task_completion.description: Navigate to the custom drug addition or formulary page in Oscar EMR.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screen showing the Oscar EMR web interface for adding or managing custom drugs/medications.
The text 'Menthol 1% Cream' is visible inside the Brand Name input field.
The text 'Menthol' is visible inside the Generic Name input field.
The text '1%' is visible inside the Strength input field.
The text 'Cream' is entered or selected in the Dosage Form field.
The text 'Topical' is entered or selected in the Route field.

task_completion.description: Save or submit the new custom drug entry.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A click on a 'Save', 'Submit', or 'Add' button, or a confirmation message indicating the drug was successfully added to the formulary.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Oscar EMR web interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser displaying the Oscar EMR application, not a terminal or script editor.

integrity.description: The agent entered the data into the actual application form.
integrity.visual_evidence: The input fields for the drug details are part of the Oscar EMR web page, not a mock-up or text file.

integrity.description: The agent did not use direct database commands to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of terminal windows showing SQL commands, Docker exec commands, or database scripts being executed to insert the drug.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct values to verify in the drug entry form are: Brand Name: 'Menthol 1% Cream', Generic Name: 'Menthol', Strength: '1%', Dosage Form: 'Cream', and Route: 'Topical'.
